description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular solar panel array elevated above a patterned base by four angled metal support legs. The solar panel surface is composed of a grid of photovoltaic cells, likely made from glass and silicon, and is supported by a robust metallic frame. The base platform is decorated with geometric black, white, and gray patterns and includes several raised and recessed sections, possibly for electrical equipment or maintenance access. Two blue-tinted rectangular areas on the base may represent access hatches or equipment covers. The structure is designed for outdoor urban environments, providing renewable energy while occupying a relatively compact footprint. The overall design suggests durability and stability, with the solar array positioned to maximize sunlight exposure.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array on a patterned base with angled metal supports and blue-tinted access areas, designed for urban solar energy generation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array, angled supports, and two blue-tinted access areas on the patterned base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the patterned base, and the arrangement of the support legs beneath the solar panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array, angled supports, and additional geometric features on the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the patterned base, blue-tinted access areas, and the underside of the solar panel array.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum support legs
attribute_array_configuration: single large rectangular array, approximately 1x8 grid of panels
